flashcards.back: A relation R on set A is reflexive if every element is related to itself: ∀a ∈ A, (a,a) ∈ R

flashcards.front: What is the difference between symmetric and antisymmetric properties?
flashcards.back: Symmetric: if (a,b) ∈ R then (b,a) ∈ R. Antisymmetric: if (a,b) ∈ R and (b,a) ∈ R then a = b. They are not opposites - a relation can be both or neither.

flashcards.front: Give an example of a transitive relation.
flashcards.back: The relation 'is less than' (<) on real numbers: if a < b and b < c, then a < c

flashcards.front: What three properties define an equivalence relation?
What three properties define a partial order?
